events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to assess compliance for block/brick laying. The observed field condition: "ladder reinforcement spacing exceeded". The work is rough-complete. Reference: TMS 402/602. We need to decide pass/fail/needs_more_info, risk, etc.

Ladder reinforcement spacing: In masonry, joint reinforcement (ladder type) is placed in mortar joints at specified vertical spacing, typically every 16 inches or as per structural drawings. TMS 402/602 (Building Code Requirements for Masonry Structures) specifies requirements for joint reinforcement. If spacing is exceeded, that means the vertical distance between reinforcement layers is greater than allowed. This is a non-compliance. The work is rough-complete, so it's installed but not yet closed out. This is a defect that could compromise structural integrity. So decision: fail. Risk: high or critical? Exceeding reinforcement spacing could lead to cracking, reduced load capacity. Probably high risk. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 80%? But we need a number. I'll estimate 80% as rough-complete. Value: not applicable. Sound source: not asked. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time, rate, order, workable: not relevant. Findings: "Ladder reinforcement spacing exceeds maximum allowed per TMS 402/602." Actions: "Remove and reinstall masonry to correct reinforcement spacing, or provide engineered remediation." Rationale: "Exceeding reinforcement spacing violates structural requirements, compromising wall strength and crack control." References: "TMS 402/602 Section on joint reinforcement spacing."

I'll output JSON accordingly.
